dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (20-period, standard 2 SD) for entry extremes and reversion to middle band (1 SD equivalent). Mapped bb_lower/-2SD for long entries and bb_upper/+2SD for short entries. Exit conditions use bb_middle as the ±1 SD reversion target. The strategy is context-dependent: goes long on oversold (lower band) and short on overbought (upper band) conditions. Backtest performance note: Sharpe 3.0 without costs, -3.0 with 1bp slippage indicates high sensitivity to transaction costs.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (20-period, standard 2 SD) for entry extremes and reversion to middle band (1 SD equivalent). Mapped bb_lower/-2SD for long entries and bb_upper/+2SD for short entries. Exit conditions use bb_middle as the ±1 SD reversion target. The strategy is context-dependent: goes long on oversold (lower band) and short on overbought (upper band) conditions. Backtest performance note: Sharpe 3.0 without costs, -3.0 with 1bp slippage indicates high sensitivity to transaction costs.

source_rule_text: A mean-reverting strategy on ES that enters on Bollinger Band extremes (±2 standard deviations) and exits when price reverts toward the moving average (±1 standard deviation).
